hook.text: Where are you really from? You're so articulate! Can I touch your hair? Each comment is tiny. Together, they're erosion.

buildup.text: Microaggressions are brief, everyday slights that communicate hostile or negative messages to marginalized groups.

discovery.text: Derald Wing Sue categorized them: microinsults, microinvalidations, and microassaults. Each works differently.

twist.text: Any single incident seems trivial. But research links cumulative microaggressions to anxiety, depression, and burnout.

climax.text: Dismissing microaggressions as oversensitivity ignores the weight of experiencing them hundreds of times per year.

punchline.text: One raindrop is nothing. A thousand a day is a flood.

quiz.question: What are microaggressions?
options: Brief everyday slights communicating negative messages
